bugs.description: Calling min() on an empty generator when no dp[j][k] is finite causes a runtime error, preventing correct handling of cases where the answer should be -1.
bugs.triggering_input: Any test case where it is impossible to obtain k fixed points, e.g., n=5 k=2 a=[5,5,5,5,4] (expected output -1).

bugs.description: Calling min() on an empty generator when no dp[j][k] is finite causes a runtime error, preventing correct handling of cases where the answer should be -1.
bugs.triggering_input: Any test case where it is impossible to obtain k fixed points, e.g., n=5 k=2 a=[5,5,5,5,4] (expected output -1).

bugs.description: The DP transition uses three nested loops (over j, prev, and c) resulting in O(n^2 * k) time complexity. With n up to 2000 and k up to n, this can be ~8 billion operations, causing a time‑limit exceed on large inputs.
bugs.triggering_input: n = 2000, k = 2000 (or any large n with k close to n)

bugs.description: The DP transition uses three nested loops (over j, prev, and c) resulting in O(n^2 * k) time complexity. With n up to 2000 and k up to n, this can be ~8 billion operations, causing a time‑limit exceed on large inputs.
bugs.triggering_input: n = 2000, k = 2000 (or any large n with k close to n)

bugs.description: DP transition uses the previous max d (dp[prev][c]) instead of the current element's required deletions (d_j). This underestimates the needed deletions, producing too small answers.
When no feasible selection of k elements exists, the generator in the min() call is empty, raising a ValueError and crashing the program.
DP transition uses the previous max d (dp[prev][c]) instead of the current element's required deletions (d_j). This underestimates the needed deletions, producing too small answers.
When no feasible selection of k elements exists, the generator in the min() call is empty, raising a ValueError and crashing the program.
Calling min() on an empty generator when there is no feasible solution causes a ValueError, crashing the program instead of outputting -1.
bugs.triggering_input: Any test case where it is impossible to obtain k fixed points (e.g., n=5, k=5, a=[5,5,5,5,5])

bugs.description: Calling min() on an empty generator when there is no feasible solution causes a ValueError, crashing the program instead of outputting -1.
bugs.triggering_input: Any test case where it is impossible to obtain k fixed points (e.g., n=5, k=5, a=[5,5,5,5,5])

bugs.description: When no valid sequence exists, the generator in the min() call is empty, causing a ValueError. The code does not handle the case where there is no dp[j][k] < INF, leading to a runtime crash instead of printing -1.

When no valid sequence exists, the generator in the min() call is empty, causing a ValueError. The code does not handle the case where there is no dp[j][k] < INF, leading to a runtime crash instead of printing -1.

aggregated_bugs.triggering_input: n = 2000, k = 2000 (or any large n with k close to n)
